title: claytonia calyptrata
intro: Claytonia calyptrata, also known as American winter purslane, is a perennial plant native to North America, characterized by its small white flowers and edible leaves. The plant is rich in vitamins A, C, and E, as well as minerals such as calcium and iron, making it a valuable source of nutrients. Claytonia calyptrata has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat conditions like scurvy, anemia, and digestive issues, due to its high vitamin C content and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant can be prepared as a tea, poultice, or salve, and is often used to soothe skin irritations, promote wound healing, and support immune function.
